bfbtools 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • The bfbtools Algorithms

    Distance from a count-vector to the nearest BFB-admitting vector

    bfb_distance_weighted.Rd

    Convenience wrapper around nearest_bfb returning a single numeric "distance" (-log(weight): 0 for an exact match, increasing as the observed vector requires more/larger corrections to admit a BFB schedule). This is on the same scale as a per-position negative log-likelihood under the chosen error model, not the Canberra distance an earlier, exponential-search-based implementation of this package used – the two are not numerically comparable, though they are both zero exactly when the input already admits BFB.

    bfb_distance_weighted(
  n_vec,
  model = c("poisson", "none"),
  min_weight = 1e-06,
  max_frontier = 2e+05
)

    Arguments

    n_vec

    observed integer count-vector, telomere to centromere.

    model

    error model: "poisson" (default; observed counts modeled as noisy Poisson draws around the true count) or "none" (no error tolerated – equivalent to admits_bfb, provided for consistency/testing).

    min_weight

    search cutoff: candidates whose cumulative weight would fall below this value are pruned (default 1e-6). Lower values search further from the observed vector at the cost of more work.

    max_frontier

    safety cap on the number of live candidates tracked at once; returns NA (with a warning) rather than growing unboundedly if this is hit (default 200000).

    Value

    a single numeric, or NA if nothing was found (see nearest_bfb).

    Examples

    bfb_distance_weighted(c(14, 7, 18, 16, 9, 12))
#> [1] 0.09270324

    Contents

    Developed by Giovanni Santacatterina.
